message:
fix(tools): chunked base64 encoding for large buffers and FindTool cwd fix

Two fixes in the agent tool infrastructure:

1. crypto_shim.rs — chunked base64 encoding:
   The JS crypto shim's hexToBuffer .toString('base64') used
   `String.fromCharCode(...this)` which applies the spread operator
   to the entire byte array as function arguments. For buffers larger
   than ~65k bytes this exceeds the engine's max call stack / argument
   limit and throws a RangeError. Now processes bytes in 4096-byte
   chunks via String.fromCharCode.apply(null, chunk), accumulating
   the binary string incrementally before calling btoa().

2. tools.rs — two changes:
   - ReadTool: remove mid-read file-growth size check. The initial
     size limit check before opening is sufficient; the mid-stream
     check caused spurious errors on files that legitimately grew
     during concurrent writes (common with agent-generated content).
   - FindTool: set .current_dir(&self.cwd) on the fd/find subprocess
     so relative path arguments resolve from the tool's working
     directory rather than the process cwd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
